4	export interface OutputFile {

6	  folderOverride?: string

9	// Keyed by firstPage of each output section. firstPage 1 is always present.
10	type OutputFilesMap = Map<number, OutputFile>
11	
12	export interface OutputFilesHandle {
13	  all: OutputFilesMap
14	  getSplitPoints: () => Set<number>
15	  toggle: (afterPage: number, prefillName: string) => boolean
16	  setName: (firstPage: number, name: string) => void
17	  pickFolderOverride: (firstPage: number) => Promise<void>
18	  reset: (firstPageName: string) => void
19	}
20	
21	export function useOutputFiles(): OutputFilesHandle {
22	  const [files, setFiles] = useState<OutputFilesMap>(new Map([[1, { name: '' }]]))
23	
24	  const toggle = (afterPage: number, prefillName: string): boolean => {
25	    const firstPage = afterPage + 1
26	    const adding = !files.has(firstPage)
27	    setFiles(prev => {
28	      const next = new Map(prev)
29	      if (next.has(firstPage)) {
30	        next.delete(firstPage)
31	      } else {
32	        next.set(firstPage, { name: prefillName })
33	      }
34	      return next
35	    })
36	    return adding
37	  }
38	
39	  const setName = useCallback((firstPage: number, name: string) => {
40	    setFiles(prev => {
41	      const entry = prev.get(firstPage)
42	      if (!entry) return prev
43	      return new Map(prev).set(firstPage, { ...entry, name })
44	    })
45	  }, [])
46	
47	  const pickFolderOverride = useCallback(async (firstPage: number) => {
48	    const folder = await PickFolder()
49	    if (!folder) return
50	    setFiles(prev => {
51	      const entry = prev.get(firstPage)
52	      if (!entry) return prev
53	      return new Map(prev).set(firstPage, { ...entry, folderOverride: folder })
54	    })
55	  }, [])
56	
57	  const reset = useCallback((firstPageName: string) => {
58	    setFiles(new Map([[1, { name: firstPageName }]]))
59	  }, [])
60	
61	  const getSplitPoints = () => {
62	    const result = new Set<number>()
63	    for (const firstPage of files.keys()) {
64	      if (firstPage > 1) result.add(firstPage - 1)
65	    }
66	    return result
67	  }
68	
69	  return {
70	    all: files,
71	    getSplitPoints,
72	    toggle,
73	    setName,
74	    pickFolderOverride,
75	    reset,
76	  }
77	}

message.content: Could duplicateFirstPages be a part of UseOutputFiles?

content.text:
The tricky part is that `useOutputFiles` doesn't know the default `outputFolder` — it only stores per-file `folderOverride`. There are two clean ways to handle it:

1. **Pass `outputFolder` as a parameter** to `useOutputFiles` so it can compute the effective folder per file and derive `duplicateFirstPages` internally
2. **Expose a function** `getDuplicates(outputFolder) => Set<number>` from the hook, leaving the `useMemo` call to the caller

Option 1 is cleaner at the call site — `duplicateFirstPages` just appears on the handle like any other derived value. Option 2 avoids coupling the hook to the default folder state.

Which do you prefer?
